title: Sales Manager (Marine Lubricants)
description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain a strong sales pipeline by identifying and acquiring new customers, while growing existing accounts.

  • Achieve and exceed targets for revenue, profitability, and sales growth.

  • Contribute to regional business strategy, market planning, and annual business cycles.

  • Deliver value-added customer service and tailor innovative lubricant solutions to meet client needs.

  • Create and implement detailed account plans for current and potential customers and distributors.

  • Provide accurate sales forecasts, performance commentaries, and activity reports.

  • Manage accounts and credit processes in line with contractual terms and company policies.

  • Collaborate with the technical services team to provide customer support and advice.

  • Gather and leverage market intelligence to keep management informed of trends, competition, and customer sentiment.

  • Monitor regional budgets and ensure financial targets and controls are met.

  • Track customer feedback to enhance satisfaction while maximizing profitability.

  • Ensure full compliance with company HSSEQ, ethical standards, and internal procedures.

Requirements

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Maritime studies, or a related field.

  • 1–2 years of sales experience, preferably within the marine, lubricants, or related industrial sectors.

  • Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.

  • Results-driven with strong problem-solving, negotiation, and communication skills.

  • Knowledgeable about current market trends and competitive business strategies.

  • Familiarity with marine lubricants and supply chain operations is an advantage.

  • Strong business acumen and excellent presentation skills.

  • Valid Class 3 Singapore Driver’s License (local travel required).

  • Willingness to travel regionally for business and client meetings.

  • Able to work from our Tuas office.

  • Fluent in written and spoken English; proficiency in Mandarin or Hindi is highly preferred.

postedCompany.description:
At Direct Search Asia we deliver innovative and flexible recruitment solutions for partners primarily within the shipping, marine & offshore, oil & gas and logistics sectors. We treat businesses and individuals equally - sharing information, challenging assumptions, maintaining open communications and operating with integrity.

Wherever you plan for your career or business to be, we have a plan to help get you there.

Visit us at www.directsearchasia.com/vacancies and follow us on LinkedIn for more information and opportunities
